requirement.original: 开 cycle: 3 项（hash chain + message validation + CC lint）+ optional audit log 迁移
requirement.clarified: Implement 4 enterprise-commit-hardening items in commit.sh + commit.md + commit-smoke.sh: M1 hash chain trailer (6 cycle artifacts as SHA-256 trailers in commit message body, git-interpret-trailers compatible, task-flow ordering, idempotent, omit-not-zero on missing artifacts); M2 message-vs-evidence validation (tightened regex set anchored to QA/AC/test context, four refuse-on-non-pass sentinels, mode skip matrix for force/force-rescue/force-manifest/bridge plus env override); M3 Conventional Commits type lint (whitelist feat|fix|refactor|docs|test|chore|build|ci|perf|style|revert, scope and breaking marker allowed, bridge skip plus env override, force modes still apply); S1 audit log default relocation (CLAUDE_LOG_DIR resolved through readlink -f to non-tmpfs filesystem; HOME/.claude is itself on tmpfs in this environment so the naive default change does not suffice).
requirement.what: Add 3 audit/integrity validators to commit.sh + 1 default-path change; document each as DOC-14..DOC-17 in commit.md; extend commit-smoke.sh with 3 new positive/negative paths l/m/n.
requirement.why: Close audit-tampering gap (M1: cycle artifacts can be mutated post-commit and commit message says nothing about it); close LLM-hallucination gap (M2: -m can claim QA PASS without qa-report backing); enforce CC type-prefix discipline (M3: prevent 'improve:' / 'chores:' / bare-subject commits); make audit JSON survive reboot (S1: tmpfs default loses forensic data).

included: M1 hash chain trailer with git-interpret-trailers semantics (--if-exists replace --if-missing add — iter-1 OBJ-1 fix), task-flow ordering, idempotent re-runs (pre-strip clause for dedup hardening — iter-1 minor fix), missing-artifact omission, bridge-mode no-op, env override CLAUDE_COMMIT_SKIP_HASH_TRAILERS, absolute temp-path requirement (iter-1 minor fix)

M2 message guard scoped to MESSAGE_SOURCE==caller only (iter-1 OBJ-4 fix — auto-message helper is deterministic, skipped with audit value 'skipped (auto-message)'), tightened anchored regex set + QA/test-anchored additions (iter-1 minor fix), four-sentinel JSON parse semantics, warning+qualifier same-paragraph proximity rule, mode skip matrix (force/force-rescue/force-manifest/bridge), env override CLAUDE_COMMIT_SKIP_MESSAGE_GUARD, subject+body scan. Audit emission contract: refusal is stderr+exit-2 ONLY, no audit JSON (iter-1 OBJ-3 fix); enumeration drops 'refused (...)'

M3 CC type lint with whitelist regex, scope/breaking-marker allowance, bridge skip, env override CLAUDE_COMMIT_SKIP_TYPE_LINT, MESSAGE_SOURCE==auto APPLIES (iter-1 codex round 2 F8 reversed the OBJ-4 propagation; iter-2 B3 confirms — M3 is universal CC discipline; auto-helper output is CC by construction per F8 + iter-2 B2 helper-side scope sanitization contract at auto-commit-message.sh:115-121), force modes still apply when caller -m, known-limitation note for git default Revert subject + merge-subject doc note (iter-1 minor fix). Audit emission contract: refusal is stderr+exit-2 ONLY, no audit JSON (iter-1 OBJ-3 fix); enumeration drops 'refused (...)' AND drops 'skipped (auto-message)' (iter-2 B3 — M3 never skips on auto-message)

S1 CLAUDE_LOG_DIR default changed to hard-coded /var/lib/claude/commit-audit (iter-1 OBJ-5 fix — warn-and-keep REMOVED); operator override accepted when readlink -f resolves non-tmpfs; tmpfs override refused with exit 2 unless CLAUDE_AUDIT_PERSIST_DISABLED=1 opt-in (records 'audit_persist: disabled (operator-acknowledged)' in JSON); mkdir-failure refuses with exit 2 (no silent tmpfs fallback); mkdir -p with mode 700

commit.md DOC-14 DOC-15 DOC-16 DOC-17 in same change set; DOC-14 verifier-contract clause (iter-1 minor fix); DOC-15 caller-only clause (iter-1 OBJ-4); DOC-16 merge-subject note (iter-1 minor fix); DOC-17 hard-default + escape clause (iter-1 OBJ-5)

commit-smoke.sh paths l (M1 trailer presence), m (M2 guard fires — fixture -m updated to CC-valid 'feat(commit): test message with all 8 ACs met PASS claim' per iter-1 OBJ-2 fix), n (M3 lint fires) added to Path summary
excluded: Trailer hard gate (refusing commits that lack the 6 trailers)
Extension of M3 whitelist with wip/merge/release/hotfix/deps/security
Diff to the 4 always-on safety layers (privilege-guard, bulk-detector, disable-model-invocation, grant manifest)

success_criteria: Trailers on a closed-task commit are git-interpret-trailers parseable: `git log --format=%(trailers:key=Ticket-SHA256) HEAD` returns the digest line
Re-running the wrapper with unchanged tree yields the same trailer block (no duplication, no stacking — pre-strip + replace combo)

Operator-supplied -m messages claiming verification are accepted iff qa-report.qa.status is pass (or warning+qualifier with same-paragraph proximity); auto-generated messages skipped with audit message_guard: 'skipped (auto-message)'
Non-CC type prefixes are refused with the documented stderr error; refusal is stderr+exit-2 only, no audit JSON written
Audit JSON path is /var/lib/claude/commit-audit/ by default (ext4 in this env); tmpfs CLAUDE_LOG_DIR refused unless CLAUDE_AUDIT_PERSIST_DISABLED=1 opt-in
10 existing smoke paths still pass; 3 new paths l/m/n pass (path m fixture -m updated to CC-valid subject so M2 actually runs)
constraints: set -euo pipefail in commit.sh; error paths must guard reads of optional artifacts
4 always-on safety layers MUST stay untouched (AC7)
All new env overrides documented in DOC-14..DOC-17

root_cause_analysis.symptom: commit.sh at baseline 640a427d59 lacks 4 audit/integrity properties: (a) commit message says 'QA PASS' but cycle artifacts can be mutated post-commit with no detection; (b) commit message can hallucinate verification claims; (c) commit subject is not type-disciplined; (d) audit JSON is on tmpfs and vanishes on reboot.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: commit.sh was iteratively grown from a closed-task semantic-commit wrapper. The 4 gaps reflect features that were never specified (M1, M3) plus an environmental side effect of the dot-claude symlink architecture (S1) plus an LLM-class concern that did not exist when the wrapper was first written (M2). None of the 4 are regressions; all are enterprise hardening over the existing surface.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A - enterprise hardening, not a regression. Baseline 640a427d59d9417b7bce5b1ee01e84c3b4715e20 represents the surface that codex's review of 2026-05-11 audited.
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Original wrapper scope was 'closed-task semantic commit with closure evidence + private-index CAS + audit JSON'. Trailers, message-claim validation, and CC lint were not in the original requirement; tmpfs default was inherited from CLAUDE_HOME convention.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Modern audit threat model includes post-commit artifact mutation (M1) and LLM-class hallucination in agent-authored messages (M2). Type discipline (M3) is a portability concern for downstream review tooling. tmpfs default (S1) loses forensic data on reboot.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Codex review on commit 640a427 produced 8-defect superset on 2026-05-11; orchestrator filtered with user to 4 honest fixes (this cycle's M1-M3 + S1); 5 ceremony items (GPG/signal/replay/Co-Auth/hard-gate) rejected.
observations: commit.sh:134 sets CLAUDE_LOG_DIR default to ${CLAUDE_HOME}/logs (observed: Read of commit.sh)
CLAUDE_HOME resolves to $HOME/.claude which is /root/.claude (observed: tool-policy hook env)
/root/.claude is a symlink to /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude (observed: readlink -f /root/.claude in BA session)
df -T /root/.claude returns tmpfs (observed: df -T /root/.claude in BA session, 2026-05-11T20:28Z)
commit.sh:1978 invokes git commit-tree with $COMMIT_MSG verbatim, no trailer append (observed: grep + Read of commit.sh:1960-2000)
commit.sh:342-413 parses -m, enforces presence, but no scan for verification-claim regexes or type prefix (observed: Read of commit.sh:200-413)
commit-smoke.sh:37-49 lists paths a..k (observed: Read of commit-smoke.sh:37-49); paths l/m/n do not exist
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: User and orchestrator named the 3 files explicitly in the dispatch prompt; BA confirmed each insertion site via grep + Read of commit.sh (matching CLAUDE_LOG_DIR / commit-tree / CALLER_MESSAGE / -m parsing line numbers) and commit-smoke.sh (Path summary block).
root_cause_analysis.located_source: hooks/commit.sh:134 (S1), hooks/commit.sh:1978 (M1), hooks/commit.sh:342-413 (M2/M3 argv path), hooks/commit.sh:2071-2103 (M1 audit mirror)

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet:
if [ "$MODE" = "force" ]; then
  echo "commit.sh: --force bypasses closure/task-id/dev-report checks; semantic planning still uses private-index/CAS audit" >&2
fi
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Operator audit warning that force mode bypasses the 3 closure layers but keeps the 4 always-on layers. M2/M3 must integrate without weakening this warning.

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet:
if [ "$MODE" = "force" ]; then
  if [ "$HAS_CALLER_MESSAGE" -eq 0 ] || [ -z "$CALLER_MESSAGE" ]; then
    echo "commit message required (-m); agent must summarize session intent" >&2
    exit 2
  fi
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Enforces -m presence for force/closed-task and forbids -m for bridge. M2 inserts AFTER this block but BEFORE commit-tree; M3 inserts at the SAME logical layer (just before run_private_index_commit) or at the end of argv parse.

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet:
if [ "${#plan_files[@]}" -gt 0 ] && [ "$plan_source" != "force-rescue" ]; then
  staged_overlap="$(git ... diff --cached --name-only -- "${plan_files[@]}" | LC_ALL=C sort -u)"
  if [ -n "$staged_overlap" ]; then
    echo "commit.sh: semantic plan touches path(s) already staged by another session ..."
    return 2
  fi
fi
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Prevents shared-index ownership violation. M1 inserts trailer logic AFTER write-tree and BEFORE commit-tree; must not perturb this guard.
Defines the artifact path conventions M1 must hash and M2 must read for qa-report.qa.status

setup.reason: non-UI -- pure backend hook + CLI shell script + smoke-test harness + agent-prompt doc; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, or (4) screenshot evidence

novelty_check.rationale: First attempt at these 4 items. Codex review on 640a427 produced an 8-defect superset; orchestrator filtered with user to 4 (M1+M2+M3+S1). Earlier 20260511-070000 and 20260511-094500 cycles addressed unrelated architect/scope work.

context.codebase_state: On branch master, dot-claude at 640a427d59d9417b7bce5b1ee01e84c3b4715e20 (pushed to origin). commit.sh = 2614 lines; commit.md = 374 lines; commit-smoke.sh = 590 lines.
context.recent_commits: 640a427 = baseline this cycle hardens against. Prior 3 commits handle inspector framework + close.md path schemes + style-inspector standard amendments (Cycle 4/5 of spec-20260503-091826). None of those commits touched commit.sh, commit.md, or commit-smoke.sh in a way that conflicts with this cycle's 4 items.

dependencies.git_interpret_trailers: Available in git >= 2.32 (long-supported). Dev should confirm via `git interpret-trailers --help` on the target host.
dependencies.jq: Used by existing close-verdict.py and audit-helper paths; M2 may rely on jq OR substitute a Python json.load equivalent (commit.sh already embeds Python heredocs; either is acceptable).

development_approach.strategy: Insert 3 new validators + 1 default change at the 4 located insertion sites in commit.sh; mirror each in commit.md as DOC-14..DOC-17; extend commit-smoke.sh Path summary + 3 new path implementations. No diff to the 4 always-on safety layers. M1 should prefer git-interpret-trailers for idempotency and RFC 822 compliance; hand-roll only if unavailable. M2's regex set is the codex-revised tightened anchored set, not the original draft.
development_approach.validation_approach: Run commit-smoke.sh end-to-end; verify 10 prior paths still pass and 3 new paths l/m/n pass. Dogfood verification at cycle-close: this cycle's own /commit invocation exercises M1+M2+M3 against this cycle's own artifacts. QA can additionally run `git log --format=%(trailers) HEAD` on the dogfood commit to confirm git-interpret-trailers parseability.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Iter-0 codex (round 1): flagged 6 in-scope real bugs and confirmed 3 design decisions — all 13 points landed in iter-0 spec. Iter-1 codex (round 2, post-QA-rejection): pending invocation after iter-1 spec finalization (this BA cycle).
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Iter-0: all 13 points incorporated. Iter-1: separate section iter1_codex_consult below records the post-iter-1 codex round.

iter1_changes.summary: Resolution of 5 blocker objections + 7 minor findings from /root/docs/dev/ba-qa-report-20260511-180000.json (verdict: fail).

objections_resolved.fix: line 91 amended from --if-exists addIfDifferent to --if-exists replace --if-missing add (matches lines 92, 273); added pre-strip dedup-hardening clause (single awk/sed pass over temp file dropping lines matching canonical POSIX regex ^(Ticket|Context|Dev-Report|QA-Report|Close-Report|Completion)-SHA256:[[:space:]]* with zero-or-more whitespace tail per iter-1 codex round 2 F1 + iter-2 B4 — matches both 'Token: <value>' and 'Token:<value>' before invoking interpret-trailers).
objections_resolved.justification: addIfDifferent stacks old+new values when artifact contents change — defeats M1's tamper-evidence property. replace+pre-strip guarantees exactly-one-per-token under both clean and dirty input states.

objections_resolved.fix: AC6 path m -m changed from 'all 8 ACs met PASS' to 'feat(commit): test message with all 8 ACs met PASS claim'. CC-valid subject passes M3 (token feat in whitelist); M2 then fires on the embedded 'all 8 ACs met' substring matching the existing anchored regex; fixture qa-report .qa.status='fail' triggers the documented claims-verification refusal.
objections_resolved.justification: M3 inserts at commit.sh:413 (end of argv-parse), M2 inserts at commit.sh:2596 (after closure-evidence). Ordering is M3 first, M2 second. Non-CC subject short-circuits at M3 before M2 runs. CC-valid embedding satisfies M3 first so M2 actually gets exercised.

objections_resolved.fix: dropped 'refused (...)' from both message_guard and type_lint audit-emission enumerations. Refusal path is now stderr+exit-2 ONLY with NO audit JSON (consistent with commit.sh:2103+ audit-JSON write site running AFTER git commit-tree). Enumerations (post-iter-2 B3 reflecting F8 reversal): message_guard ∈ {skipped (force|force-rescue|force-manifest|bridge|env|auto-message), applied (no claim found|claim matches)}; type_lint ∈ {skipped (bridge|env), applied (accepted <type>)} — M3's 'skipped (auto-message)' value REMOVED per iter-1 codex round 2 F8 + iter-2 B3; M3 applies universally including on auto-helper output.
objections_resolved.justification: audit JSON is built at commit.sh:2103+ AFTER commit-tree returns; refusal path exits 2 before reaching the heredoc. The spec previously contracted an emission the implementation cannot produce. Dropping 'refused' from the enumeration is option (b) in QA's framing — refusal stderr + exit-2 is already self-evidencing.

objections_resolved.fix: M2 restricted to fire ONLY when MESSAGE_SOURCE==caller (the operator/agent supplied -m at commit.sh:381 force or 400 closed-task-with--m). When MESSAGE_SOURCE==auto (closed-task at line 396 invoked auto-commit-message.sh), M2 skips with audit message_guard: 'skipped (auto-message)'. M3's iter-1 OBJ-4 draft proposed the same parallel-skip but iter-1 codex round 2 F8 REVERSED that — M3 is universal CC discipline (not LLM-hallucination-scoped); auto-helper output passes M3 by construction (F8 ctype whitelist membership + iter-2 B2 helper-side scope sanitization at auto-commit-message.sh:115-121). Iter-2 B3 confirms: M3 applies on MESSAGE_SOURCE==auto with audit type_lint: 'applied (accepted <type>)'.
objections_resolved.justification: User's requirement (ticket line 11) explicitly frames the gap as -m messages that LLM-hallucinate. auto-commit-message.sh is a deterministic shell script; it cannot hallucinate. Refusing an auto-derived message would surprise the operator. M3 is a separate concern (first-line CC discipline); universal application catches future helper regression.

objections_resolved.fix: S1 default hard-picked to /var/lib/claude/commit-audit/. Warn-and-keep REMOVED. New code paths: (a) no override → /var/lib/claude/commit-audit/ (ext4); (b) override to non-tmpfs → use it; (c) override to tmpfs without CLAUDE_AUDIT_PERSIST_DISABLED=1 → exit 2; (d) override to tmpfs with CLAUDE_AUDIT_PERSIST_DISABLED=1 → use it, record audit_persist: 'disabled (operator-acknowledged)' in JSON; (e) mkdir-failure → exit 2; no silent tmpfs fallback in any path.
objections_resolved.justification: QA confirmed / is ext4 on /dev/sda1 with 460GB free. /var/lib/ is FHS-conformant and filesystem-agnostic. Hard-default eliminates the AC4 internal contradiction (warn-and-keep was satisfying the fallback clause while failing the THEN clause).

minor_findings_resolved.fix: addressed in OBJ-1 fix — pre-strip clause added to M1 trailer mechanic

extended M2 regex set with three additions: \b(QA|qa-report)\s+(cleared|approved|signed[\s-]?off)\b, \b(all\s+)?tests?\s+(are\s+)?(passed|passing|green|clear)\b, \b(everything|all)\s+(looks?|tests?|checks?)\s+(good|passed|fine)\b; explicitly excluded ship-it / good-to-go / no-blockers (release-readiness, not QA verification)
M3 section adds explicit merge-subject doc note; DOC-16 documents that git-generated Merge branch '<name>' subjects are NOT exempted; operators reformat to chore(merge): or use env override
AC8 mandates DOC-14 carry verbatim verifier-contract clause: verifiers rehash live artifact files on disk (sha256sum docs/dev/qa-report-<task-id>.json), NOT the tree-blob

AC1 idempotency tested via direct trailer-helper invocation twice on temp message fixture with pre-existing duplicate trailers; assert exactly-one-per-token and fresh-digest. Does not depend on full wrapper closure path.

AC1 qa-report-absent clause enumerates fixture artifacts: ticket, context, dev-report, close-report-PRIMARY (required by commit.sh:2418-2434), completion; intentionally omits qa-report; -m must be CC-valid claim-free subject

verification.evidence_basis: OBJ-2: Read of commit.sh:342-413 (argv-parse end) + commit.sh:2395-2434 (closure-detection) confirms M3 at 413 fires before M2 at 2596. New subject matches CC regex with token feat. OBJ-3: Read of commit.sh:2095-2160 confirms audit heredoc runs after commit-tree. OBJ-4: Read of commit.sh:376-406 confirms MESSAGE_SOURCE is set to 'caller' at 381/400 and 'auto' at 396. OBJ-5: live df -T / returned /dev/sda1 ext4 460GB; live df -T /root/.claude returned tmpfs; ls -ld /var/lib/ confirmed drwxr-xr-x 51 root root on /dev/sda1.

iter1_codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex round 2 returned 9 substantive points: F1 (M1 pre-strip regex inconsistency + zero-whitespace gap), F2 (OBJ-2 confirmed clean), F3 (AC6 path m requires PRIMARY close-report fixture), F4 (OBJ-3 audit enum drop confirmed clean), F5 (MESSAGE_SOURCE skip ordering risk), F6 (S1 preflight must run pre-commit-tree, not at audit write site), F7 (container persistence overclaim — non-tmpfs at commit-time is necessary but not sufficient for long-term retention), F8 (M3 auto-message skip is new scope drift introduced in iter-1 — M3 is general CC discipline, not LLM-scoped), F9 (AC1.qa_report_absent confirmed clean).

in_scope_real_bug: F1 — M1 pre-strip regex unified to canonical POSIX with zero-or-more whitespace
F3 — AC6 path m needs PRIMARY close-report fixture (qa-report-fail alone cannot reach M2)
F6 — S1 preflight at commit.sh:2400 area pre-commit-tree, not at audit JSON write site
F8 — M3 auto-message skip removed (M3 is universal CC discipline; auto-helper output is CC by construction at auto-commit-message.sh:123)
in_scope_minor: F5 — mode skip matrix evaluation order mandated (bridge → force → force-rescue → force-manifest → env → MESSAGE_SOURCE → caller)
F7 — DOC-17 container caveat documented (no additional code check)
confirmed_clean: F2 — OBJ-2 subject passes M3 and triggers M2 regex match
F4 — OBJ-3 'refused' enum drop is correct; remaining refusal text is stderr+exit-2 behavioral, not audit enum
F9 — AC1.qa_report_absent fixture correctly uses PRIMARY close-report path

iter1_codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: 4 in_scope_real_bug items fixed in ticket (F1 pre-strip canonical regex; F3 AC6 path m close-report; F6 S1 preflight relocation; F8 M3 universal restoration). 2 in_scope_minor items fixed (F5 evaluation order; F7 DOC-17 caveat). 3 confirmed-clean points recorded with no action. Codex sub-test (live git interpret-trailers experiment) empirically validated F1.
iter1_codex_consult.verdict_drove: iter-1 spec hardened: 5 OBJs from iter-0 QA report PLUS 6 new codex round 2 findings all incorporated. Spec was expected to pass BA-QA iter-2 review but QA iter-1 (via codex round 3) surfaced 4 new blockers (B1-B4) requiring iter-2.

iter2_changes.summary: Resolution of 4 NEW blocker objections (B1-B4) from /root/docs/dev/ba-qa-report-iter1-20260511-180000.json (verdict: fail; codex round 3) plus 2 in_scope_real_bug items (C-iter2-1, C-iter2-2) from this BA-self codex round 4 consultation. Iter-2 is FINAL allowed per 3-max policy.

iter1_blockers_resolved.fix: Relocated S1 preflight from commit.sh:2400 (closure block, unreachable for force modes) to INSIDE run_private_index_commit (function starts at commit.sh:1455, preflight inserted after `local plan_source` at :1463, BEFORE `local tmp_dir` at :1465, well BEFORE git commit-tree at :1978). Single chokepoint covers all 6 invocation paths verified by codex round 4: closed-task PRIMARY (:2613 dev-report plan), closed-task SECONDARY (:2613 qa-report fallback), closed-task+manifest (:2610), force (:2394), force-rescue (:2391), force-manifest (:2385). Bridge mode does NOT call run_private_index_commit and is exempt by construction.
iter1_blockers_resolved.justification: Function-body anchor is reached by every code path producing a commit object. Closure-block anchor at :2400 is reached only by closed-task paths, skipped entirely by force-mode dispatch at :2366-2395. Preflight must run pre-commit-tree for ALL paths to satisfy AC4's 'NO commit object created' clause.
touchpoints: ticket M1/S1 section line 154-160 (insertion recipe + why-not-at-2400 + audit-write-site contract)
F6 retro line 380 (annotated with iter-2 reversal)

iter1_blockers_resolved.fix: Added helper-side scope-sanitization contract for auto-commit-message.sh:115-121. Helper MUST apply scope = re.sub(r'[^A-Za-z0-9_-]', '', stem.replace('_','-'))[:24] or 'task' before scope is substituted into the subject string. Co-lands in same cycle with M3 lint. DOC-16 carries the contract verbatim.
iter1_blockers_resolved.justification: F8's 'helper output passes M3 by construction' verified ctype whitelist membership but did not verify scope character safety. M3's \([^)]+\) scope regex rejects `)`; helper derives scope from Path.stem which may contain `)` for unusual filenames (legacy artifacts). Sanitization closes the construction-hole; co-landing avoids a transitive failure window.
touchpoints: ticket M3 F8 rationale (helper-side scope-sanitization contract)
context.json where/in_user_path/affected_files (C-iter2-2 — helper added to all three machine-readable lists)
DOC-16 helper sanitization clause in AC8
